reviews.review_msg: Awsome prof! Lectures where actually super helpful and the midterm/final tests where all related to the slides. The practice questions she gives during lecture/ practice are great. She is super approachable and understanding, great prof!

VERYY lecture heavy so make sure to always be up to date with the slides and such. Overall decent lecturer but her exams can be quite difficult and sometimes too difficult. One question of the final was worth 6 marks and only shows up in one lecture slide with just a few sentences for that topic. So pay attention always to everything she teaches.
amazing lecturer, very approachable, and very nice. grading was rough because of the ta's but if you want exams to be remarked, she's more than willing and awesome! would take her course again.

Overall, an amazing professor, would definitely recommend taking Bisc 102 with her. Lectures are great I'd advise you to take notes to understand the main concepts. Lab exam is straightforward open book so pay attention and do your lab work. Exams are fair do practice exams and questions she uploads plus cue cards, etc. and you're dialled.

Very caring and kind prof and the tests are very fair and reasonable if you put in some work. Even then cramming is possible because the lectures are so well organized, but if you actually take a few hours a week it's an easy A especially for the lecture component. The labs depend on the TA marking which is unpredictable, but not bad.

Super nice, literally an angel, im shocked at the past reviews. Gives good hints to test questions in lecture, tests are reasonable but weighed heavy. Focus on the learning objectives and vocabulary for written answers. Was always open to feedback and very approachable.
if u ever feel down, just remember if we survived megan barker we can survive anything.
Arrogant, demeaning, towards students. Vague and unorganized. Avoid at all costs. Should not be anywhere near teaching.

Elizabeth has a bad attitude towards her students. She was cold and curt when you talked to her. She made labs uncomfortable because she was rude and condescending when you asked for help or clarification. She was easily upset and let her temper flare in class. Her marking was unreasonably difficult, inconsistent, and slow.

She was extremely rude and made multiple vicious comments about student's abilities in class and in private emails. We paid good money to learn biology, not to be personally criticized about our intelligence. It's unfortunate that she refuses to take responsibility for her lack of clarity and bizarre, fluctuating expectations.
